overview.Summary: Operation Dust Storm is a cyber campaign targeting the communications sector through a phased attack involving phishing, remote command and control, and a disruptive DDoS attack.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of communication infrastructure to impair critical services.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: An adversary targets mobile phone users with a phishing attack for the purpose of soliciting account passwords or sensitive information from the user.
stage.description: The attackers initiate the attack by sending phishing emails containing malicious links to employees of the communications company. These links lead to a fake login page designed to steal credentials, exploiting human error and social engineering techniques.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 555 Name: Remote Services with Stolen Credentials Description: This pattern of attack involves an adversary that uses stolen credentials to leverage remote services such as RDP, telnet, SSH, and VNC to log into a system.
stage.description: Once initial access is gained via phishing, the attackers establish a remote command and control (C2) channel using stolen credentials. This allows them to issue commands and execute malicious activities within the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 488 Name: HTTP Flood Description: An adversary may execute a flooding attack using the HTTP protocol with the intent to deny legitimate users access to a service by consuming resources at the application layer such as web services and their infrastructure.
stage.description: The attackers launch a DDoS attack using an HTTP flood technique, overwhelming the target's communications infrastructure with excessive requests, rendering it unavailable to legitimate users.

Scenario.summary: Operation Dust Storm employs a multi-stage attack strategy starting with phishing to gain initial access, followed by establishing remote command and control, and culminating in a disruptive DDoS attack to impact the communications infrastructure.
